query:
we should file a gh issue for this: Do 1 and 2, skip 3.

  Option 2 implementation note: don't pass the vector index into select_source_candidates and call get_embedding
  (that's O(N) per entry, called during selection = expensive). Instead, compute a HashSet<u64> of IDs with
  embeddings once before selection — the vector index's internal id_map.entry_to_data already has exactly this set
  as a byproduct of its structure. Pass the set, check membership O(1) per candidate. The no-embedding entries
  disappear from tier 1 permanently with zero runtime cost per tick.

  Option 1: shuffle both tiers independently before take(max_sources). This gives non-deterministic progress through
   the 2900+ real isolated candidates. Some ticks will find edges, most won't, but the tick is no longer stuck.

  Option 3: not worth it yet. The combination of shuffle + no-embedding filter already eliminates both root causes.
  A bloom filter adds schema or in-memory state for a benefit that's marginal once shuffle is in place — you're
  trading occasional redundant NLI calls for persistent complexity. Revisit if it turns out a large fraction of
  ticks still score 0 after 1+2 land.

  One thing to flag: even with both fixes, the 0.7 NLI threshold against 2356 lesson-learned entries is structurally
   hard. Lesson-learned entries are event narrations ("when X happened, do Y") — they don't entail each other in the
   NLI sense even when semantically related. Expect the graph to fill well among the 988
  decision/pattern/procedure/convention entries and remain sparse in the lesson-learned corpus at this threshold.
  That's not a bug — it's the threshold doing its job. The graph cohesion trend will show this:
  cross_category_edge_count rises, but isolated_entry_count plateau is higher than the entry count might suggest.

  File this as a follow-up to #412. Options 1 and 2 are a bugfix-protocol candidate — small, targeted, no design
  change beyond what was already scoped.

entries.title: Tick source-candidate selection with stable ordering stalls after first window is exhausted
Tick passes over full entry set: bound, shuffle, and embedding-filter source candidates independently of NLI pair cap
embedded_ids HashSet and rand::rng() must be built in async context, not inside rayon closure
